response.step_by_step_log: 1. 00:00-01:56: Detected a vocal performance by Suara and virtual character Hakui Koyori. Identified the song as 'Fuantei na Kamisama' from 'Utawarerumono: Mask of Deception' through on-screen text and audio. 2. 01:57-05:00: Identified as conversational segment without music. 3. Searched for lyrics using '不安定な神様 Suara lyrics' and successfully found original lyrics. 4. Generated English translation for the detected lyrics.
response.global_summary: A video featuring a live musical performance of the song 'Fuantei na Kamisama' by Suara and virtual character Hakui Koyori, followed by a conversation between the two performers about the 'Utawarerumono' series.

scenes.description: The performance of 'Fuantei na Kamisama'. Suara and Hakui Koyori sing and dance on stage. Visuals from the 'Utawarerumono' series are projected on screen.
scenes.reference_lyrics_og:

あなたが泣いている
流るる涙の雨
泣くなとは言わない
心かなしくて

生きとし生けるもの
何かを背負いながら

どれほど救われただろう
あどけない笑顔は
なくしたくはない

運命

探すため引き裂かれても

scenes.reference_lyrics_en:
Unstable God
Repeating meetings and partings
Until the day we can meet again
A traveler chasing time

You are crying
A rain of flowing tears
I won't say 'don't cry'
My heart is sad

All living things
While carrying something
Are connected by invisible chains

How much have I been saved?
Your innocent smile
I don't want to lose it

Unstable God
Repeating past and future
Born and dying
Fate

In a world crossed incorrectly
Repeating meetings and partings
Until the day we can meet again
A traveler chasing time

An innocent heart
While being dyed black
Cannot be suppressed
And is unleashed

How many times did I call your name?
Your innocent smile
Is no longer anywhere

Unstable God
Inherited dreams and love
Raising, protecting, and entrusting
Fate

In a world crossed incorrectly
Even if torn apart to search
Believing that we can meet again
A traveler chasing time

Unstable God
Repeating past and future

Fate

scenes.description: The song ends. Suara introduces herself, and the two performers continue a conversation about their live performance and the 'Utawarerumono' game.
